subsection.title: Midpoint rule in one variable
subsection.p: Here \Delta x=\frac{b-a}{N}, and x_i^* is the midpoint of the ith subinterval.

subsection.title: Midpoint rule over a rectangle
subsection.p: For R=[a,b]\times[c,d], \iint_R f(x,y)\,dA \approx \sum_{i=1}^{M}\sum_{j=1}^{N} f(x_i^*,y_j^*)\,\Delta x\,\Delta y.

program.input:
def midpoint_double(f, a, b, c, d, M=200, N=200): dx = (b - a) / M dy = (d - c) / N
x = a + (np.arange(M) + 0.5)*dx y = c + (np.arange(N) + 0.5)*dy
X, Y = np.meshgrid(x, y) return np.sum(f(X, Y))*dx*dy

subsection.title: Region under variable bounds
subsection.p: For a\le x\le b, \qquad g_1(x)\le y\le g_2(x), \iint_R f\,dA = \int_a^b\int_{g_1(x)}^{g_2(x)}f(x,y)\,dy\,dx.
Using dblquad:

program.input:
from scipy.integrate import dblquad
value, error = dblquad( lambda y, x: f(x, y), a, b, lambda x: g1(x), lambda x: g2(x) )

subsection.title: Polar numerical integration
subsection.p: If x=r\cos\theta, \qquad y=r\sin\theta, then dA=r\,dr\,d\theta.
So \iint_R f(x,y)\,dA = \iint_{\widetilde R} f(r\cos\theta,r\sin\theta)\,r\,dr\,d\theta.
Code pattern:

program.input: value, error = dblquad( lambda r, theta: f(r*np.cos(theta), r*np.sin(theta))*r, theta0, theta1, lambda theta: r0(theta), lambda theta: r1(theta) )

subsection.title: Triple integral over a box
subsection.p: For B=[a,b]\times[c,d]\times[e,f], \iiint_B F(x,y,z)\,dV \approx \sum F(x_i^*,y_j^*,z_k^*)\,\Delta x\,\Delta y\,\Delta z.

subsection.title: Triple integral with variable bounds
subsection.p: For a\le x\le b, g_1(x)\le y\le g_2(x), h_1(x,y)\le z\le h_2(x,y), \iiint_E F\,dV = \int_a^b\int_{g_1(x)}^{g_2(x)} \int_{h_1(x,y)}^{h_2(x,y)} F(x,y,z)\,dz\,dy\,dx.
Using tplquad:

program.input:
from scipy.integrate import tplquad
value, error = tplquad( lambda z, y, x: F(x, y, z), a, b, lambda x: g1(x), lambda x: g2(x), lambda x, y: h1(x, y), lambda x, y: h2(x, y) )

subsection.p: Again, the innermost variable comes first.

subsection.title: Cylindrical numerical integration
subsection.p: Use the integrand F(r\cos\theta,r\sin\theta,z)\,r.

subsection.title: Spherical numerical integration
subsection.p: Use the integrand F(\rho\sin\phi\cos\theta,\rho\sin\phi\sin\theta,\rho\cos\phi) \rho^2\sin\phi.

subsection.title: Monte Carlo estimate
subsection.p: For a region R inside a box B: \iint_R f\,dA \approx \operatorname{Area}(B) \cdot \frac1N \sum_{i=1}^N f(x_i,y_i)\mathbf 1_R(x_i,y_i).
Code pattern:
Monte Carlo is usually slow but useful in irregular regions or high dimensions.

subsection.title: Common warnings
subsection.p: Numerical integration returns an approximation and often an error estimate.
Singularities require special care.
Highly oscillatory functions require finer sampling.
Coordinate changes require Jacobian factors.
Improper integrals may appear finite on a finite sample but still diverge.
